feedback.details: mmdc validates successfully (exit 0) with no syntax errors. Line 6-73 contains valid C4Container syntax. Minor deduction: WCAG 2.1 AA color contrast compliance for default C4Container styling (blue #1168BD) is not explicitly verified or overridden with WCAG-compliant colors.

Missing distinct 'Orchestrator Service' container - 'Real-time Routing Container' (line 16) appears to be a different architectural concept. The PRD explicitly requires an 'Orchestrator Service' that manages the routing workflow. While most containers are present (API Gateway line 9, Model Router line 17, Local Model Instances lines 19-23, Frontier API Adapter line 24, Monitoring Service line 26, Health Check Service line 25, Administrative Interface line 29, Persistent Storage lines 30-31, External System boundary line 7), the missing Orchestrator Service represents a fundamental architectural gap. Also, the External System boundary (line 7) encompasses all containers including llm-switch internal containers, which contradicts C4 Level 2 conventions where External System should only contain truly external dependencies (Nomad cluster, Consul, Vault, model servers, etc.).

All relationships use solid lines (Rel statements), but the contract requires dashed lines for asynchronous relationships. Asynchronous flows that incorrectly use solid lines: Model Router → Langfuse trace collector (line 52), Langfuse → Offline Self-Learning (line 53), Offline Self-Learning → Model Router (line 54), telemetry pulls (lines 55-58), and Dead Letter Queue write (line 72). These MUST use Rel() with dashed styling to distinguish from synchronous flows. Additionally, the self-learning trace flow is indirect (Model Router → Langfuse → Offline Self-Learning) rather than the direct 'Model Router → Self-learning system' implied by the contract language.

Network segmentation exists with DMZ (line 8) and Internal VPC (line 11) boundaries, but no explicit firewall boundary visualized between the public-facing API Gateway (line 9) and internal containers. The contract requires 'firewall boundaries' to be shown. Golang labels (lines 9-29), bifrost usage (lines 43, 16-18), Nomad deployment topology (lines 13-14 with GPU/Memory/node pool constraints), and Consul/Vault integration points (lines 30-31, 64-65) are present. Hardware telemetry integration (lines 16, 19-23 with GPU/CPU metrics) is correctly shown.

Word count of 198 (line 3) falls within the 150-250 threshold. The narrative covers container purposes, bifrost messaging, mTLS encryption, and hardware telemetry. However, it lacks explicit description of 'how the diagram supports the developer and operations journeys from the PRD' - a specific requirement. The narrative mentions 'self-learning traces' and 'configuration artifacts' but does not explain the developer experience of zero-code-change model integration or the operations engineer experience of monitoring and incident response.

Horizontal scaling is demonstrated with load balancers (Qwen LB line 18, Nemotron LB line 21) routing to pools of identical adapters (lines 19-20, 22-23). Configuration artifacts (Nomad Job line 13, Consul Config line 14) are visually separated in a boundary (line 12). However, the diagram fails to explicitly demonstrate that 'adding a new LLM model must be shown as a configuration change...without touching application containers' - this is only implied through the presence of Nomad job specs, not explicitly visualized as a workflow or relationship.

Almost entirely missing from the visual diagram. (1) No fallback paths shown: when Qwen or Nemotron fail, there are no visual arrows showing requests routing to alternate models. (2) Circuit breaker pattern only exists as text in Model Router label (line 17: 'implements...circuit breaker') but no visual indicator (e.g., dashed fallback arrows or circuit breaker symbol). (3) Dead Letter Queue (line 32) is present with 'After 3 retries' relationship (line 72), which is correct, but the intermediate retry logic and automatic rerouting are not visualized. (4) Health check bidirectional flow (line 71) exists but automatic rerouting behavior is not shown.

Security zones partially implemented: DMZ (line 8) and Internal VPC (line 11) boundaries exist, but no firewall visualized between them. Authentication flows (mTLS) shown only in protocol labels (lines 43-72) rather than as explicit security relationship annotations. Cost differentiation exists via 'Low Cost' (lines 45-46) and 'High Cost' (line 47) labels. VRAM utilization tiers shown at lines 37 (24GB Qwen) and 38 (48GB Nemotron). However, 'VRAM utilization tiers affecting routing decisions' is not explicitly shown as a decision flow or annotated on the Model Router relationships.

overall_summary: Round 3 shows incremental improvement over Rounds 1-2, particularly in Mermaid validity and basic container completeness. However, the diagram remains critically deficient in Failure Handling Visibility (5.5/10) and Relationship Accuracy (6.0/10). The most egregious issues are: (1) all relationships use solid lines, violating the contract requirement that asynchronous flows must be dashed; (2) no visual fallback paths or circuit breaker patterns exist, only text labels claiming their existence; (3) missing distinct 'Orchestrator Service' container; (4) lack of explicit firewall boundaries between DMZ and Internal VPC. The narrative meets word count (198) but lacks explicit developer/operations journey descriptions. To pass, the next iteration must: convert async relationships (lines 52-54, 55-58, 72) to dashed styling, add visual fallback arrows from Model Router to alternate models on failure, add explicit firewall boundary between lines 8 and 11, add distinct Orchestrator Service container, and explicitly show how new models are added via Nomad configuration.
